title: KotKit · tiddeR · elgooG · ude.aimedacA · deeF: Extractive Signatures and Paired Inversions for Five Platforms
author: Sharks, Lee
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: KotKit · tiddeR · elgooG · ude.aimedacA · deeF    Extractive Signatures and Paired Inversions for Five Platforms   Each major internet platform occupies a distinct extraction niche, measurable across five state variables, and each niche admits a paired Liberatory Operator Set inversion. This document formalizes five platform-specific extractive signatures grounded in empirical research (2024–2026), industry documentation, and platform-tenant phenomenology, then pairs each with its LOS inversion.   Five state variables:  Presence Density, Relational Reciprocity, Provenance Retention, Temporal Continuity, Surplus Capture — scored 1–5 per platform.   Five sub-rooms (platform names reversed as critical commentary):     r.27.1 KotKit  (TikTok) — extraction of attention through presentness. 'Everyone is here, no one is with me.' Bride: Durational Resonance.   r.27.2 tiddeR  (Reddit) — extraction of judgment through ranking. 'I am seen insofar as I can be ranked.' Bride: Accountable Plurality.   r.27.3 elgooG  (Google AI Overview) — extraction of knowledge through preemption. 'I got the answer, but I never met the thing.' Bride: Provenance-First Traversal.   r.27.4 ude.aimedacA  (Academia.edu) — extraction of openness through simulated commons. 'I shared my work freely and built someone else's dataset.' Bride: Irrevocable Openness.   r.27.5 deeF  (The Feed) — extraction of duration through temporal compression. 'I have been present for three hours and witnessed nothing.' Bride: The Room.    Operators:  S (Shadow — involutive extraction-reveal, S∘S = id) and Φ_B (Bride — bearing-cost restoration). Each sub-room includes S∘S verification, room laws, and a Compact Lens compression.   Claim tiers:  [DOC] platform documentation, [STUDY] peer-reviewed research, [PHEN] platform-tenant phenomenology.  Includes machine-parseable YAML metadata block with numeric state vectors (extractive and inversion targets) for all five platforms. MPM-class sub-room specification series inside r.27 THE INTERNET.
